varieties.name: 🧬 HYBRID = First-generation (F1) cross between distinct parent lines for improved traits
French Gray Shallot (Griselle) 🏆
varieties.origin: France (Central/Southwest Asia origin), cultivated for centuries
varieties.traits: Small to medium elongated bulbs with tough, gray outer skin; curved shape; 1-2 inches diameter
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yields, 6-10 bulbs per plant; smaller than other varieties

varieties.name: French Red Shallot 🏆
varieties.traits: Elongated teardrop shape; reddish-brown to mahogany copper skin; 1.5-2.5 inches long
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent yields, 8-12 bulbs per plant

varieties.name: Zebrune (Cuisse de Poulet du Poitou) 🏆
varieties.origin: Western France (Poitou region), heirloom from Eastern Europe
varieties.traits: Long, curved banana/drumstick shape; 3-4 inches length; golden-brown skin with faint zebra-like striping; rose-gold coloring
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields, large bulbs; 6-8 bulbs per plant
varieties.availability: Specialty seed suppliers; Australian heirloom seed companies; online international suppliers

varieties.name: Jersey (Pink) Shallot 🏆
varieties.origin: Jersey Island (off coast of France), grown in USA since 1820s
varieties.traits: Large, round to teardrop shape; glossy rose-pink to coppery skin; 2-3 inches diameter
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent yields, larger bulbs than most varieties; 8-10 bulbs per plant

varieties.name: Hative de Niort 🏆
varieties.traits: Slender, tapered bulbs; tough reddish-brown skin; elongated shape
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yields; early maturing (80-90 days)

varieties.name: Roderique (Echalion) 🏆
varieties.traits: Long, narrow, uniform torpedo shape; tall bulbs; copper-brown skin; 3-4 inches length
varieties.yield_notes: Very good yields; uniform bulbs; stores exceptionally well

varieties.name: Ambition F1
varieties.traits: Traditional round blocky shape; reddish-copper to brown skin; 2-3 inches diameter; uniform size
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent yields; produces large single or double bulbs; uniform harvests

varieties.name: Conservor F1
varieties.traits: Elongated teardrop shape; reddish-copper to dark rosy skin; 2-3 inches; single bulb formation
varieties.yield_notes: High yields; predominantly single large bulbs rather than clusters; uniform size

varieties.name: Innovator F1
varieties.traits: Round traditional shape; single bulb formation; medium-large size
varieties.yield_notes: High yields; uniform bulbs; resistant to splitting

varieties.name: Matador F1
varieties.traits: Round to slightly elongated; reddish skin; medium size; uniform shape
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields; reliable harvests; single to double bulb formation
varieties.availability: Australian seed companies; specialty vegetable suppliers

varieties.name: Dutch Yellow Shallot
varieties.traits: Golden-tan papery skin; rounder, slightly larger than French types; 2-2.5 inches diameter
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields; tight clusters; stores well (5-6 months)

varieties.name: Dutch Red Shallot
varieties.traits: Red to copper skin; round to oval shape; medium size; clustered growth
varieties.yield_notes: Multiplies well; 6-10 bulbs per plant; reliable yields
varieties.availability: Common in Australian nurseries; widely available

varieties.name: Golden Shallot
varieties.traits: Medium to large bulbs; attractive golden color with pinkish tinge beneath outer skin; 2-3 inches
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields; 8-12 bulbs per plant; reliable multiplier

varieties.name: Pikant
varieties.traits: Small to medium; mahogany-brown skin; slightly elongated; 1.5-2 inches
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yields; quick-maturing (approximately 80 days); smaller bulbs

varieties.name: Yellow Potato Onion 🏆
varieties.origin: Historical variety pre-1790, widely grown in Australian home gardens late 1800s-early 1900s
varieties.traits: Large flattened bulbs; yellow-brown skin; can reach 4 inches diameter but typically 1-2 inches; remains enclosed in seed bulb skin longer than shallots
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent multiplier; 1 bulb produces 6-12 new bulbs; larger bulbs than shallots; drought-tolerant

varieties.name: Green Mountain Multiplier
varieties.origin: USA (Utah), developed by Kelly Winterton from chance seedling, 21st century introduction
varieties.traits: Very large bulbs; white to cream skin; 3-4+ inches possible; significantly larger than traditional potato onions
varieties.yield_notes: Exceptional yields; produces very large bulbs from seed; dividing variety

varieties.name: French Winter Red Potato Onion 🏆
varieties.traits: Squat bulbs; dark copper skin; trifurcated (3-part) bulbs; medium size
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields; cold-hardy; excellent storage

varieties.name: Evergreen Hardy Bunching (Allium fistulosum)
varieties.traits: Doesn't form bulbs; grows as green bunching onions; tall green stalks; white lower portions
varieties.yield_notes: Continuous harvest; cut-and-come-again; produces offsets
varieties.availability: Widely available Australian nurseries; Succeed Heirlooms Australia; common in Queensland

varieties.name: Sambar Onion (Small Onion/Chinna Vengayam) 🏆
varieties.origin: Indigenous to South India, particularly Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, and tropical monsoon climates; cultivated for centuries
varieties.traits: Very small bulbs (thumb-width to marble size, 15-25mm diameter); spherical to oval shape; bright red-purple skin; intense color
varieties.yield_notes: Multiplies prolifically; many small bulbs; adapted to tropical conditions

varieties.name: Madras Onion (Red Shallot/Chinna Ulli) 🏆
varieties.origin: Indigenous to Southern India, especially Tamil Nadu and coastal regions
varieties.traits: Small red-purple bulbs; oval to round; 20-30mm; bright reddish-brown papery skin
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent tropical yields; well-adapted to heat and monsoon

varieties.name: Rose Onion (Krishnapuram Onion) 🏆
varieties.origin: Karnataka (Kolar District) and Andhra Pradesh (Cuddapah/Kadapa district), South India
varieties.traits: Small to medium; rose-pink to reddish skin; round to slightly oval; 25-35mm diameter
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields in tropical climate; heat-adapted

varieties.name: Podisu Shallot 🏆
varieties.origin: Tamil Nadu, Maharashtra, Pondicherry region, Indigenous cultivar
varieties.traits: Small spherical to oval bulbs; bright red-purple color; 20-30mm; traditional tropical type
varieties.yield_notes: Prolific in tropical conditions; monsoon-adapted

varieties.name: Bawang Merah (Indonesian Red Shallot) 🌱
varieties.origin: Indonesia, Malaysia, Southeast Asia; traditional tropical variety
varieties.traits: Small, intensely colored purple-red bulbs; 15-30mm diameter; very aromatic; compact
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent in tropical heat; prolific multiplier; adapted to monsoon conditions

varieties.name: Thai Shallot (Hom Dang) 🌱
varieties.origin: Thailand, Southeast Asia; traditional tropical cultivar
varieties.traits: Small oblong bulbs with tapered ends; bright red papery skin; 20-35mm; 2-3 cloves per bulb like garlic
varieties.yield_notes: Prolific in tropical heat; well-adapted to monsoon conditions

varieties.name: Tropical Red Shallot (Asian Type)
varieties.origin: Southeast Asia, adapted throughout tropical regions
varieties.traits: Small to medium; deep red-purple skin; oval shape; 25-35mm; tropical-adapted
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent yields in tropical heat and humidity

varieties.name: Bima Brebes
varieties.traits: Medium-sized; red skin; adapted to tropical conditions; uniform shape
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields; proven in tropical floating cultivation systems

varieties.name: Red Asian Multiplier
varieties.traits: Small to medium red bulbs; clustered growth; 25-40mm; multiplying type
varieties.yield_notes: Multiplies well in tropical heat; monsoon-tolerant
varieties.availability: Asian seed suppliers; tropical monsoon climates agricultural markets; South Indian distributors

varieties.name: Ambition F1 (Tropical Selection)
varieties.traits: Round blocky shape; copper-red skin; 30-40mm; uniform bulbs
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields even in tropical heat; single to double bulb formation

varieties.name: French Red (Tropical Trial) 🏆
varieties.origin: France origin, some adaptation to warm climates possible
varieties.traits: Elongated copper-brown bulbs; 40-50mm; traditional French shape
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yields in tropical conditions; better in cooler season
